heading: Loan forgiveness for teachers
label: 1078–10 Loan forgiveness for teachers
notes: References in TextSection 9101 of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965, referred to in subsecs. (b)(1)(B) and (g)(3), was amended by and, as so amended, is now section 8101 of the Act and no longer defines “highly qualified”. A reference in this section to the term “highly qualified” as defined in section 9101 of the Act is to be treated as a reference to such term under such section 9101 as in effect on the day before the date of enactment of . See , set out as a Use of the Term “Highly Qualified” in Other Laws note under .Pub. L. 114–95Pub. L. 114–95section 9214(a)(1) of Pub. Subtitle D of title I of the Act is classified generally to division D of subchapter I (§ 12601 et seq.) of chapter 129 of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare. children.heading: Statement of purpose
children.label: a Statement of purpose
children.text: It is the purpose of this section to encourage individuals to enter and continue in the teaching profession.

children.label: A
children.text: in a school or location that qualifies under for loan cancellation for Perkins loan recipients who teach in such schools or locations; andsection 1087ee(a)(2)(A) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: if employed as an elementary school or secondary school teacher, is highly qualified as defined in section 9101 of the Elementary Secondary Education Act of 1965 [], or meets the requirements of subsection (g)(3); and1220 U.S.C. 7801See References in Text note below.1So in original. Probably should be preceded by “and”.2
is not in default on a loan for which the borrower seeks forgiveness.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall repay not more than $5,000 in the aggregate of the loan obligation on a loan made under section 1078 or 1078–8 of this title that is outstanding after the completion of the fifth complete school year of teaching described in subsection (b)(1). No borrower may receive a reduction of loan obligations under both this section and .section 1087j of this title

children.heading: Treatment of consolidation loans
children.label: 2 Treatment of consolidation loans
children.text: A loan amount for a loan made under may be a qualified loan amount for the purposes of this subsection only to the extent that such loan amount was used to repay a Federal Direct Stafford Loan, a Federal Direct Unsubsidized Stafford Loan, or a loan made under section 1078 or 1078–8 of this title for a borrower who meets the requirements of subsection (b), as determined in accordance with regulations prescribed by the Secretary.section 1078–3 of this title

children.heading: Additional amounts for teachers in mathematics, science, or special education
children.label: 3 Additional amounts for teachers in mathematics, science, or special education
i
children.text: who meets the requirements of subsection (b); and

children.label: ii
children.text: whose qualifying employment for purposes of such subsection is teaching mathematics or science on a full-time basis; and

children.label: i
children.text: who meets the requirements of subsection (b);

children.label: ii
children.text: whose qualifying employment for purposes of such subsection is as a special education teacher whose primary responsibility is to provide special education to children with disabilities (as those terms are defined in ); andsection 1401 of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: who, as certified by the chief administrative officer of the public or non-profit private elementary school or secondary school in which the borrower is employed, or, in the case of a teacher who is employed by an educational service agency, as certified by the chief administrative officer of such agency, is teaching children with disabilities that correspond with the borrower’s special education training and has demonstrated knowledge and teaching skills in the content areas of the elementary school or secondary school curriculum that the borrower is teaching.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: d Regulations
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to issue such regulations as may be necessary to carry out the provisions of this section.

children.heading: Construction
children.label: e Construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to authorize any refunding of any repayment of a loan.

children.heading: List
children.label: f List
children.text: If the list of schools in which a teacher may perform service pursuant to subsection (b) is not available before May 1 of any year, the Secretary may use the list for the year preceding the year for which the determination is made to make such service determination.

children.label: A
children.text: meets the requirements of subsection (b)(1)(A) in any year during such service; and

children.label: B
children.text: in a subsequent year fails to meet the requirements of such subsection,

children.label: A
children.text: ;section 1078–11 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: ; orsection 1087e(m) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: subtitle D of title I of the National and Community Service Act of 1990 ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 12601

children.heading: Private school teachers
children.label: 3 Private school teachers
children.text: An individual who is employed as a teacher in a private school and is exempt from State certification requirements (unless otherwise applicable under State law), may, in lieu of the requirement of subsection (b)(1)(B), have such employment treated as qualifying employment under this section if such individual is permitted to and does satisfy rigorous subject knowledge and skills tests by taking competency tests in the applicable grade levels and subject areas. For such purposes, the competency tests taken by such a private school teacher shall be recognized by 5 or more States for the purpose of fulfilling the highly qualified teacher requirements under section 9101 of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 [], and the score achieved by such teacher on each test shall equal or exceed the average passing score of those 5 States.120 U.S.C. 7801

children.heading: “Year” defined
children.label: h “Year” defined
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “year”, where applied to service as a teacher, means an academic year as defined by the Secretary.
